questions.question: What does the 'echo' command in Unix do?
options: Display the current date and time
Display the current username
Display text on the screen
Display the current working directory
questions.explanation: The 'echo' command in Unix displays text on the screen.

questions.question: What does the 'printf' command in Unix do?
options: Display the current date and time
Display the current username
Format and display text on the screen
Display the current working directory
questions.explanation: The 'printf' command in Unix formats and displays text on the screen.

questions.question: What does the 'ls' command in Unix do?
options: Display the current date and time
Display the current username
List files and directories in the current directory
Display the current working directory
questions.explanation: The 'ls' command in Unix lists files and directories in the current directory.

questions.question: What does the 'who' command in Unix do?
options: Display the current date and time
Display the current username
Display information about the current user
List files and directories in the current directory
questions.explanation: The 'who' command in Unix displays information about the current user.

questions.question: What does the 'date' command in Unix do?
options: Display the current date and time
Display the current username
Calculate the current working directory
Check if the system is running
questions.explanation: The 'date' command in Unix displays the current date and time.

questions.question: What does the 'passwd' command in Unix do?
options: Display the current date and time
Display the current username
Change the current user's password
List files and directories in the current directory
questions.explanation: The 'passwd' command in Unix changes the current user's password.

questions.question: What does the 'cal' command in Unix do?
options: Display the current date and time
Display the current username
Display a calendar for the current month
List files and directories in the current directory
questions.explanation: The 'cal' command in Unix displays a calendar for the current month.

questions.question: What does 'date +m/%d/%y' command in Unix do?
options: Display the current date and time
Display the current username
Format the current date as mm/dd/yy
List files and directories in the current directory
questions.explanation: The 'date +m/%d/%y' command in Unix formats the current date as mm/dd/yy.

questions.question: What does 'ls -l | grep keyword' command in Unix do?
options: Display the current date and time
Display the current username
List files and directories in the current directory sorted by name
List all files in the current directory that contain the keyword
questions.explanation: The 'ls -l | grep keyword' command in Unix lists all files in the current directory that contain the keyword.

questions.question: What does 'echo 'Hello World!' | head -n 1' command in Unix do?
options: Display the current date and time
Display the current username
Display the first line of the echo command
List files and directories in the current directory
questions.explanation: The 'echo 'Hello World!' | head -n 1' command in Unix displays the first line of the echo command.

questions.question: What does 'ls | tail -n 5' command in Unix do?
options: Display the current date and time
Display the current username
List the last 5 files in the current directory
List all files in the current directory
questions.explanation: The 'ls | tail -n 5' command in Unix lists the last 5 files in the current directory.

questions.question: What does 'echo 'Hello World!' | sort' command in Unix do?
options: Display the current date and time
Display the current username
Sort the echo command alphabetically
List files and directories in the current directory
questions.explanation: The 'echo 'Hello World!' | sort' command in Unix sorts the echo command alphabetically.

questions.question: What does 'ls | grep keyword | wc -l' command in Unix do?
options: Display the current date and time
Display the current username
Count the number of files in the current directory that contain the keyword
List all files in the current directory
questions.explanation: The 'ls | grep keyword | wc -l' command in Unix counts the number of files in the current directory that contain the keyword.
